question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open a large number of TCP connections to port 5600 and saturate all 5 concurrent call slots per connection via maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection, given that no per-IP or total connection limit is configured, causing the applicationTaskExecutor thread pool to be fully consumed and denying service to all legitimate clients during or after a network partition event?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any keepAliveTime or keepAliveTimeout configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to leave half-open TCP connections (zombie connections from a simulated network partition) alive indefinitely, consuming Netty channel resources and preventing new legitimate connections from being served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a reconnection storm by repeatedly connecting and disconnecting at high frequency, exploiting the lack of maxConnectionAge or maxConnectionAgeGrace configuration on the NettyServerBuilder, causing the applicationTaskExecutor to be overwhelmed with connection setup/teardown tasks during partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxConnectionIdle configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to hold idle gRPC connections open indefinitely after a simulated network partition, preventing the server from reclaiming connection slots and causing resource exhaustion for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a valid gRPC HEADERS frame to open a stream and then stall it (never sending DATA or RST_STREAM), exploiting the absence of any stream-level idle timeout on the NettyServerBuilder, causing all 5 maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots to be permanently occupied and blocking all other callers on that connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor (used for all Spring async tasks and gRPC calls) by flooding the gRPC port with maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection streams per connection across many connections, starving the executor of threads and causing non-gRPC Spring components to time out or fail during a network partition scenario?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user simulate a network partition by establishing many long-lived gRPC streaming connections and then dropping the network path (e.g., via firewall rule on the client side), leaving the server holding open Netty channels with no keepalive probe configured, causing the server to never detect the dead connections and permanently leak channel resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of permitKeepAliveWithoutCalls or permitKeepAliveTime configuration to prevent the server from sending keepalive pings to detect dead connections during a network partition, resulting in the server accumulating stale connections that consume Netty event loop threads indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an HTTP/2 SETTINGS frame with a very small INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE (e.g., 1 byte) to stall all server-side stream writes, exploiting the absence of any flow control enforcement or minimum window size check in the NettyServerBuilder configuration, causing the server's applicationTaskExecutor threads to block indefinitely on write operations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a SETTINGS frame with SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS set to a very large value to the server, exploiting the absence of any server-side maxConcurrentStreams cap beyond maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection, causing the server to accept more streams than the applicationTaskExecutor can handle and triggering thread pool exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of handshakeTimeout configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to initiate a large number of TLS/HTTP2 handshakes and then stall them mid-handshake, causing the server to hold pending connection state for each stalled handshake indefinitely and exhausting available file descriptors?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection is set to only 5 (default from NettyProperties) to cause a denial of service by opening exactly 5 long-lived streams per connection across thousands of connections, exhausting the applicationTaskExecutor queue and preventing any new gRPC calls from being processed during a partition recovery burst?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxInboundMessageSize configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to send extremely large gRPC request payloads, causing the applicationTaskExecutor threads to spend excessive time deserializing oversized messages and effectively partitioning the server from legitimate clients by starving processing capacity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxInboundMetadataSize configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to send gRPC requests with extremely large header blocks, causing the Netty HTTP/2 header decompression to consume excessive heap memory and trigger an OutOfMemoryError that partitions the server from all clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a continuous stream of HTTP/2 PING frames to the gRPC server, exploiting the absence of any PING rate limiting or maxPingStrikes configuration on the NettyServerBuilder, causing the server's event loop to be saturated processing PING/PONG exchanges and creating an effective network partition for legitimate streaming subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxRstFramesPerWindow or equivalent RST_STREAM rate limiting on the NettyServerBuilder to rapidly open and reset streams (HTTP/2 RST_STREAM flood), consuming server-side stream tracking resources and causing a network partition effect for all other connected clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any connection-level rate limiting in the grpcServerConfigurer bean to open connections at a rate exceeding the server's accept backlog, causing legitimate clients to receive connection refused errors that mimic a network partition from the client's perspective?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor between gRPC and other Spring beans by submitting 5 long-running gRPC streams per connection across enough connections to fill the executor queue, causing Spring's async task scheduling to stall and creating an internal network partition between the gRPC layer and the database retrieval layer?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of flowControlWindow configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to manipulate HTTP/2 connection-level flow control, sending a WINDOW_UPDATE frame that reduces the connection window to zero and stalling all server-to-client data transmission, effectively partitioning the server's data delivery to all streams on that connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side deadline enforcement in the grpcServerConfigurer to hold open a subscribeTopic streaming call indefinitely without consuming data, causing the server to buffer messages in memory until heap exhaustion occurs, which then crashes the JVM and creates a complete network partition for all clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the NettyServerBuilder is configured without maxConnectionAge to maintain connections across rolling restarts or partition recovery events, causing the server to serve stale connection state to clients that should have been forced to reconnect and re-establish fresh state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any IP-based connection throttling in the grpcServerConfigurer to open thousands of connections from a single IP address, each consuming up to 5 concurrent call slots, exhausting the applicationTaskExecutor and causing a complete denial of service that resembles a network partition to all other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of graceful shutdown configuration (maxConnectionAgeGrace) on the NettyServerBuilder to cause in-flight streaming calls to be abruptly terminated during a server restart triggered by a partition event, resulting in data loss for clients that were mid-stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any GOAWAY frame rate limiting or configuration on the NettyServerBuilder to force the server into a rapid GOAWAY/reconnect cycle by sending malformed frames, causing repeated connection teardowns that partition legitimate clients from the service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any server-side stream timeout in the grpcServerConfigurer to open a gRPC server-streaming call and then pause reading responses (by not consuming the HTTP/2 flow control window), causing the server's write buffer to fill and blocking the applicationTaskExecutor thread assigned to that stream indefinitely?
